headline: Trump's ballroom obsession worries Republicans
description: The costly and monumental real estate project has repeatedly been mentioned in presidential speeches, highlighting the billionaire's disconnect from public concerns. Only 28% of citizens deemed the construction necessary.

headline: Israel PM says close to killing all architects of October 7 attack
'Staggering' Iran toll drives up global executions to their highest since 1981, says Amnesty International
New Hungarian PM discloses Viktor Orban's luxurious offices
description: Péter Magyar, the recently appointed prime minister of Hungary, launched a media campaign on Friday, signaling a break with his far-right predecessor, who was notorious for allowing corruption to flourish among his inner circle.

headline: Cannes: Exiled Russian filmmaker Andrey Zvyagintsev's 'Minotaur' delivers anti-war message through intimate lens
description: The 62-year-old filmmaker, now living in France, chose the conflict between his country and Ukraine as the backdrop for his latest film. He depicts the consequences of the war, which he openly condemns, within the realm of married life. Despite his open hatred of Putin's regime, his mere presence at Cannes could draw condemnation from Ukrainian advocacy groups.

headline: Ligue 1: PSG defeated by Paris FC ahead of Champions League final
description: Paris Saint-Germain lost 2-1 on Sunday ahead of the Champions League final on May 30 against Arsenal. Ousmane Dembélé came off before the half-hour in an apparent injury scare. Coach Luis Enrique said 'it has been a tricky end to the season' for PSG as they prep for 'the biggest match in our history.'

headline: Alya Aglan, historian: 'De Gaulle is difficult to interpret; he is himself an actor'
description: More than half a century after his death, General de Gaulle continues to inspire filmmakers, cartoonists and comic book authors. In an interview with Le Monde, historian Alya Aglan unpacks the driving forces behind these diverse portrayals.

headline: WHO keeps evaluation of hantavirus as 'low risk'

Le Provençal, a Riviera hotel with breathtaking views
USS Gerald Ford aircraft carrier returns home after 326-day deployment
From Paris to China, Bistrot Ordinaire takes diners on a surprising culinary journey
description: In the 11th arrondissement of Paris, self-taught chef Liang Shan has opened a bistro that is 'ordinaire' in name only. His menu boldly bridges Chinese and French cuisines, far from your typical Parisian fare.

headline: Trump, with ambitions for Mars, Canada and Greenland, tries to reclaim frontier myth of pioneer America
description: Since the very beginning, the US has based its growth on the conquest of new geographical or political frontiers. In this spirit, the current president speaks of other lands – on Earth and in space – while the rise of artificial intelligence opens up uncharted territories.

headline: After the 2025 disaster, Macron hopes to make his last year as president count
description: Emmanuel Macron, who will leave office on May 13, 2027, seeks to be remembered as a president who stayed active until the end. On the world stage, he recently regained some of the credit he lost to domestic challenges.
